channel.title: Dialogue-based CALL on Serge Bibauw
channel.description: Recent content in Dialogue-based CALL on Serge Bibauw

item.title: Task-based dialogue systems for language learning
item.description: Doctoral research project at KU Leuven and Université catholique de Louvain. Funded by a SENESCYT scholarship (Ecuador). Researcher: Serge Bibauw. Supervisors: Piet Desmet (KU Leuven) and Thomas François (Université catholique de Louvain)

item.title: Dialogue-based CALL: an overview of existing research
Conversational agents for language learning: state of the art and avenues for research on task-based agents
Insights from a multilevel meta-analysis on the effectiveness of dialogue-based CALL
item.description: Dialogue-based computer-assisted language learning (CALL) systems allow a learner to practice meaningfully a foreign language (L2) with an automated agent, whether through an oral (spoken dialogue systems) or written interface (chatbots) (Bibauw, François, & Desmet, 2015). While various dialogue-based CALL systems have been developed and tested with learners against different evaluation schemes, individual evaluations have provided limited information on their effectiveness on L2 development (Bibauw, François, & Desmet, Submitted). In order to obtain a better comprehension of their effects on L2 proficiency development, we conducted a meta-analysis on all the experimental studies measuring an impact of such systems on language learning outcomes (40 publications).

item.title: Instructional design and natural language processing in dialogue-based CALL
The effectiveness of dialogue-based CALL on L2 proficiency development: a meta-analysis
